title: DNA Extraction Protocol for Yeast PacBio Sequencing
author: Douda Bensasson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rved63e

steps.instruction: For each strain, inoculate 15 ml YPD in 50 ml Falcon tubes from single colonies on YPD plates using a sterile inoculation loop. undetermined, YPD for innoculation
Incubate at overnight at 30°C (for at least 20 hours, e.g. 24 hours). Undetermined, Incubation 20:00:00, Incubation

Take 1.4 ml of each overnight culture and add to a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. *Do this a total of 8 times for each strain (so there are 8 microcentrifuge tubes for each strain). undetermined, culture
Centrifuge at high speed (14,000 rpm on Eppendorf centrifuge or 16,162g on other) for 2 minutes to pellet the cells. 00:02:00, Centrifugation
Remove and discard the supernatant into a yeast waste jar containing Virkon solution (make this up with Virkon powder and water to a deep pink color).
Pipette to resuspend the pellet in 296 µl 50mM EDTA pH 8. undetermined, 50mM EDTA pH 8

Add 4 µl Lyticase (25U/ul, diluted stock from Sigma) and incubate overnight at 37°C. We used only 100 units of lyticase (Sigma, L2524) in an overnight incubation at 37 °C to save money (past experiments showed no change in yield). undetermined, Lyticase Undetermined, Incubation Temperature
Centrifuge the sample at 16,162g for 2 mins. 00:02:00, Centrifugation
Remove and discard the supernatant.
Add 300 µl Promega Nuclei Lysis Solution. undetermined, Promega Nuclei Lysis Solution

Add 100 µl Promega Protein Precipitation Solution. Stir with the pipette tip and pipette up and down to resuspend the pellet. The combination of Protein Precipitation Solution and Nuclei Lysis Solution makes the mixture cloudy (not the pellet or protein). undetermined, Promega Protein Precipitation Solution
Let the sample sit on ice for 5-30 minutes. 00:05:00, on ice
Centrifuge at high speed for 10 minutes. 00:10:00, Centrifugation

Carefully pipette the supernatant to a clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube containing 300 µl Isopropanol. Be careful to avoid the protein pellet and leave some residual liquid to avoid contaminating the DNA solution. undetermined, Isopropanol
Invert the samples 50 times, and a wispy mass might be visible.
Centrifuge at high speed for 10 minutes. 00:10:00, Centrifugaiton

Carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant without dislodging the pellet. Drain the tube on clean absorbent paper. At this stage, you should see a pellet for all samples except negative controls (if you see a pellet in the negatives, then this is probably not DNA and will be purified away in subsequent steps). If the pellet is dislodged during the removal of the supernatant, respin the sample. You may need to leave some residual liquid in the tube to avoid dislodging the pellet (the pellet after the ethanol wash below will be whiter and less easy to dislodge).
Add 300 μl of room temperature 70% ethanol and gently invert the tube 20 times to wash the DNA pellet. undetermined, 70% ethanol

Centrifuge at 16,162g for 5 mins. Carefully pipette off all the ethanol. Note: the pellet at this stage contains impurities, so the size (or visibility) of the pellet is not an indicator of final yield. 00:05:00, Centrifugation
Drain the tube on clean absorbent paper, and allow the pellet to air dry for 15 minutes. 00:15:00, Drying time
Add 25 µl Promega DNA rehydration solution, and gently resuspend the pellet by pipetting. undetermined, Promega DNA rehydration solution

Add 1.5 µl of Promega RNase solution and pipette to mix the RNase into each sample. Also, a 1:2 dilution of the RNase solution from Qiagen (i.e. 5.25 units, Qiagen, Cat. no. 19101) can be used in place of the Promega RNase solution. undetermined, Promega RNase solution
Incubate at 37°C for 1-2 hours. 00:01:00, hours Undetermined, Incubation
Vortex for 1 second, and spin quickly to collect liquid. 00:00:01, Vortex
Leave at room temperature overnight, then store DNA extracts in the fridge at 2-8°C until ready for ethanol precipitation (could be several days later). Undetermined, Storage
Check yield of every sample on gel. If all samples give a good yield for each strain, then it is ok to pool them all. Discard any samples where 1 µl is not visible on a gel.

Pool samples for each strain and remove RNA from purified DNA. (modified from Qiagen's protocol, Apendix D ) *I am pretty sure any ethanol precipitation protocol would be fine here. I only used the protocol and 'Protein Precipitation Solution' from Qiagen because it was to hand.
Cool DNA extracts 15 minutes on ice. 00:15:00, Cooling
Spin at high speed for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Pool the supernatants of the four extracts for each strain into a fresh tube, bringing the final volume for each strain to approximately 180 μl.
Add 20 μl (or other amounts) of Promega (or Qiagen's) DNA Hydration solution, bringing the final volume of DNA extract up to 200 μl for each strain. undetermined, Promega DNA Hydration solution
Add 6 μl RNase A from Promega (or 3 μl RNase A Solution from Qiagen), mix by pipetting, quick 1 second vortex and spin. undetermined, RNase A 00:00:01, vortex
Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour, then transfer to ice. 01:00:00, incubation Undetermined, incubation
Add 100 μl Promega (or Qiagen) Protein Precipitation Solution (0.5 volume), 400 μl 96-100% Ethanol from freezer (2 volumes).
Mix by inverting 50 times (visible mass formed in all except negatives). Leave on ice for 15 mins. 00:15:00, on ice
Centrifuge at high speed for 10 minutes. 00:10:00, Centrifugation
Carefully, pipette off supernatant.
Add 600 μl room temperature 70% ethanol (3 volumes). undetermined, 70% ethanol
Centrifuge at high speed for 3 minutes. 00:03:00, Centrifugation
Carefully, pipette off and discard supernatant and air dry the pellet for 15 minutes. 00:15:00, air dry
Resuspend pellet in 110 μl Qiagen DNA Hydration Solution (1xTE) by gentle pipetting. undetermined, Qiagen DNA Hydration Solution
Incubation at 37 ºC for 1-1.5 hours to fully dissolve the DNA. Undetermined, Incubation 01:00:00, Incubation
Quick spin, pipette to mix, 15 minutes at room temperature. 00:15:00

Centrifuge at high speed for 10 minutes in order to pellet any impurities that might have precipitated together with the DNA. The DNA should now be fully dissolved and will no longer form a pellet. 00:10:00, Centrifugation

Transfer the supernatants to new tubes (even though no pellets were visible at this stage). The quality and quantity of DNA was assessed by running alongside a high molecular weight ladder (GeneRuler High Range DNA Ladder, SM1351) on a 0.5% agarose gel stained with 1x Gel Red in 1x TAE buffer, by NanoDrop, and using a Qubit fluorometer.
